title: Company Advertising
description: Colonial companies print their own newspapers and advertise shares at inflated rates. They spin rumors into tales of guaranteed success and fabulous riches. The real profit comes from stock manipulation - dumping shares between expedition departure and the year it takes to confirm failure. Advertisements mimic Shackleton's famous expedition ad: 'Men wanted for hazardous journey. Small wages, bitter cold...'
definition-of-done: Newspaper/advertising system where companies can print propaganda. Stock manipulation mechanics tied to expedition cycles. Procedurally generated advertisement text.
testing-plan: Verify newspaper generation with procedural ad text. Test stock price manipulation mechanics. Verify timing between expedition and confirmation affects profits.
unknowns: Player participation in manipulation vs AI-only
